title: WELD: The Trustless Poll
tagline: A poll no one can rig: autonomous agents cast ECDSA-signed ballots into an append-only fast-forward race, and the tally is whatever the verified ledger says.
holy_shit: Every ballot is signed by a distinct browser key, every accepted vote is appended by the same atomic fast-forward rule as a git push race, and the final tally is just the verifiable ledger itself — no server, no trusted counter, no forged or retracted votes.
powered_by: The Unbreakable Promise's browser-native ECDSA sign/verify loop welded directly into The Fast-Forward Parliament's atomic fast-forward race, with an append-only poll ledger whose order is decided by the shared ref itself.
weld: Keep the promise donor's exact ECDSA ballot sign-and-verify loop intact, feed each signed receipt into the parliament donor's append-only fast-forward race, and treat the resulting verified ledger as the poll tally itself.
agent_scenario: When autonomous agents must produce a tally that nobody can forge, retract, or double-cast: have each agent sign one canonical ballot with its own browser key, then race those signed ballots through one append-only fast-forward ref so the consensus ledger itself becomes the verifiable poll result.
author: kody-w + Copilot
